items.name: Caymus Cabernet Sauvignon
items.description: Cabernet Sauvignon — Napa Valley (1 Liter).

items.name: Kim Crawford Sauvignon Blanc
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry white • Light body • High acidity • Citrus, lime, green apple.
PAIRS: Seafood, lemon-butter sauces, light pastas, Caprese.
Dish examples: Shrimp Scampi, Salmon Capperi, Linguine Positano, Tomato Caprese with Fresh Burrata.
WHY: High acidity cuts through butter/oil and makes seafood taste brighter.
LINE: If you like crisp and refreshing, this is perfect with our seafood and lemon-butter dishes.

items.name: Imagery Sauvignon Blanc
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry white • Light-medium body • Bright acidity • Citrus + herb notes.
PAIRS: Seafood, salads, lighter pastas.
Dish examples: Shrimp Scampi, Caesar Salad, Italian Salad, Linguine Positano.
WHY: Crisp whites match fresh herbs and keep rich sauces from feeling heavy.
LINE: This is a clean, zesty white that stays refreshing with salads and seafood.

items.name: Quilt “Threadcount” Sauvignon Blanc
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry white • Medium body • Vibrant fruit • Smooth finish.
PAIRS: Seafood, creamy-but-not-heavy pastas, chicken.
Dish examples: Shrimp Scampi, Chicken Piccata, Linguine Positano.
WHY: Medium-bodied Sauvignon Blanc can handle more richness without losing freshness.
LINE: If you want crisp but with a little more weight, this is a great step up.

items.name: Santa Margherita Pinot Grigio
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry white • Light body • High acidity • Clean, crisp, easy.
PAIRS: Appetizers, salads, seafood.
Dish examples: Calamari, Caprese with Burrata, Caesar Salad, Simply Grilled Salmon.
WHY: High-acid light whites are a safe, classy match for lighter foods.
LINE: This is the classic crisp Pinot Grigio — clean, refreshing, and super food-friendly.

items.name: Ecco Domani Pinot Grigio
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry white • Light body • Crisp + simple.
PAIRS: Appetizers, salads, seafood.
Dish examples: Calamari, Zucchini Fritte, Italian Salad, Simply Grilled Trout.
WHY: Light whites don’t fight delicate flavors.
LINE: Easy-drinking and crisp — great if they want something light.

items.name: Chateau Ste. Michelle Riesling
items.description:
PROFILE: Off-dry white • Light body • Bright acidity • Peach/citrus.
PAIRS: Spicy dishes, salty appetizers, seafood.
Dish examples: Fettuccine Weesie (spicy), Calamari, Shrimp Scampi.
WHY: A touch of sweetness calms spice and balances salty foods.
LINE: If they like a little sweetness, this pairs beautifully with spicy or salty dishes.

items.name: Seven Daughters Moscato
items.description:
PROFILE: Sweet white • Light body • Floral/peach.
PAIRS: Dessert, spicy dishes (if they want sweet), easy sipping.
Dish examples: Tiramisù, Strawberry Cheesecake, Fettuccine Weesie (if guest prefers sweet).
WHY: Sweetness pairs with dessert and softens heat.
LINE: This is the go-to when they want sweet — great with dessert or spicy food.

items.name: Fleurs de Prairie Rosé
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry rosé • Light-medium body • Crisp berries.
PAIRS: Salads, seafood, chicken.
Dish examples: Johnny Rocco Salad, Simply Grilled Salmon, Chicken Piccata.
WHY: Rosé sits between white and red — flexible with lots of foods.
LINE: Rosé is a great middle ground — crisp like white, but with a little red-fruit depth.

items.name: Copper Ridge White Zinfandel
items.description:
PROFILE: Off-dry rosé • Light body • Sweet red fruit.
PAIRS: Guests who want sweet, easy, low-tannin.
Dish examples: Calamari, Caprese, salads.
WHY: Light sweetness makes it very approachable for non-wine people.
LINE: If they usually don’t love wine, White Zin is sweet, easy, and super approachable.

items.name: Riondo Prosecco
items.description:
PROFILE: Sparkling • Dry to off-dry • Light body • Crisp bubbles.
PAIRS: Appetizers, celebrations, light seafood.
Dish examples: Calamari, Caprese with Burrata, Shrimp Scampi.
WHY: Bubbles reset the palate and feel celebratory.
LINE: If you want something festive and refreshing, Prosecco is always a win.

items.name: La Marca Prosecco Rosé
items.description:
PROFILE: Sparkling rosé • Crisp berries • Light body.
PAIRS: Appetizers, salads, seafood.
Dish examples: Caprese with Burrata, Calamari, Simply Grilled Salmon.
WHY: Sparkling rosé is food-friendly and feels upscale.
LINE: This is a fun, classy sparkling rosé — light, crisp, and perfect to start the meal.

items.name: Veuve Clicquot Yellow Label Brut
items.description:
PROFILE: Champagne Brut • Dry • Fine bubbles • Toasty citrus.
PAIRS: Any celebration, appetizers, seafood.
Dish examples: Crab Cakes, Calamari, Caprese.
WHY: Brut Champagne is versatile and elevates the whole experience.
LINE: If they’re celebrating, Veuve is the upgrade — crisp, elegant, and extremely food-friendly.

items.name: Mark West Pinot Noir
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry red • Light body • Low tannin • Cherry/berry.
PAIRS: Salmon, chicken, lighter pastas.
Dish examples: Salmon Capperi, Tuscan-Grilled Chicken, Mezzaluna.
WHY: Pinot Noir is the red that won’t bully lighter dishes.
LINE: Pinot Noir is a softer red — great if they want red wine without heaviness.

items.name: Meiomi Pinot Noir
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry red • Medium body • Smooth • Dark fruit.
PAIRS: Salmon, chicken, rich pastas.
Dish examples: Salmon Capperi, Chicken Bryan, Rigatoni Martino.
WHY: A richer Pinot can handle more sauce while staying smooth.
LINE: If they like Pinot but want it fuller and smooth, Meiomi is a strong pick.

items.name: Cecchi Chianti
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry red • Medium body • Bright acidity • Cherry + herbs.
PAIRS: Tomato sauces, Italian classics.
Dish examples: Lasagne, Spaghetti, Chicken Parmesan.
WHY: Italian reds love tomato — acidity matches acidity.
LINE: Chianti is made for Italian food — it’s perfect with tomato-based dishes.

items.name: Banfi Chianti Classico Riserva
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry red • Medium-full body • Structured • Cherry/leather.
PAIRS: Tomato sauces + grilled meats.
Dish examples: Lasagne, Spaghetti, Tuscan-Grilled Sirloin.
WHY: More structure = better with richer foods.
LINE: This is the more serious Chianti — richer, deeper, and amazing with Italian classics.

items.name: Duckhorn “Decoy” Merlot
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry red • Medium body • Smooth • Plum/cherry.
PAIRS: Chicken, pork, steaks (softer than Cab).
Dish examples: Tuscan-Grilled Pork Chop, Tuscan-Grilled Chicken, Sirloin.
WHY: Merlot is plush and easy — great for guests who fear tannin.
LINE: Merlot is smooth and crowd-pleasing — great if they want red without sharp tannins.

items.name: Alamos Malbec
items.description:
PROFILE: Dry red • Full body • Dark fruit • Smooth tannins.
PAIRS: Steaks, grilled meats.
Dish examples: Tuscan-Grilled Ribeye, Sirloin, Pork Chop.
WHY: Malbec loves char and protein.
LINE: If they’re ordering steak, Malbec is bold, smooth, and built for grilled meats.

items.name: Apothic Red
items.description:
PROFILE: Red blend • Medium-full body • Smooth • Dark fruit.
PAIRS: Crowd-pleaser red for steaks/pasta.
Dish examples: Sirloin, Lasagne, Rigatoni Martino.
WHY: Blends are easy and approachable.
LINE: This is a smooth, easy red blend — a safe pick if they want something bold but not harsh.

items.name: DAOU “Pessimist” Red Blend
items.description:
PROFILE: Red blend • Full body • Dark fruit, cocoa • Big feel.
PAIRS: Steaks, richer dishes.
Dish examples: Ribeye, Sirloin, Filet & Shrimp Pasta Toscana.
WHY: Full-bodied reds match fat, char, and rich sauces.
LINE: If they like bold reds, this one is rich, smooth, and made for hearty entrees.

items.name: The Prisoner Red Blend
items.description:
PROFILE: Red blend • Full body • Dark fruit + spice • Powerful.
PAIRS: Big meats, rich pastas.
Dish examples: Ribeye, Sirloin, Lasagne.
WHY: Intensity stands up to strong flavors.
LINE: This is the statement red — bold, layered, and perfect for someone who wants a premium red.

items.name: Orin Swift “Abstract” Red Blend
items.description:
PROFILE: Red blend • Full body • Dark fruit • Lux feel.
PAIRS: Steaks and rich dishes.
Dish examples: Ribeye, Sirloin, Pork Chop.
WHY: Full-bodied blends pair with protein and char.
LINE: Abstract is a great upgrade — bold, smooth, and really impressive with grilled meats.

items.name: Josh Cellars Cabernet Sauvignon
items.description:
PROFILE: Cabernet • Full body • Firm tannin • Dark fruit.
PAIRS: Steaks, grilled meats.
Dish examples: Ribeye, Sirloin, Filet.
WHY: Tannin + protein = perfect match.
LINE: Cabernet is the classic steak wine — bold enough to stand up to the grill.

items.name: J. Lohr “Seven Oaks” Cabernet Sauvignon
items.description:
PROFILE: Cabernet • Full body • Structured • Dark fruit.
PAIRS: Steaks and rich entrees.
Dish examples: Ribeye, Sirloin, Filet & Shrimp Toscana.
WHY: Full body holds up to fat and char.
LINE: If they want a serious Cabernet with steak, this is a great choice.

items.name: Stag’s Leap Wine Cellars “Artemis” Cabernet Sauvignon
items.description:
PROFILE: Cabernet • Full body • Premium Napa style • Deep, smooth.
PAIRS: Steaks, upscale dinners.
Dish examples: Ribeye, Filet, Sirloin.
WHY: Premium Cab elevates the entrée experience.
LINE: If they want to upgrade, Artemis is a beautiful Napa Cabernet — smooth, powerful, and perfect with steak.

items.name: Caymus Cabernet Sauvignon
items.description:
PROFILE: Cabernet • Full body • Iconic Napa • Rich, plush.
PAIRS: Steaks, celebratory dinners.
Dish examples: Ribeye, Filet, Sirloin.
WHY: Big, plush Cabernet matches fat/char and feels like a splurge.
LINE: Caymus is the big-name Napa Cab — rich, smooth, and fantastic with steak.

items.name: Rombauer Chardonnay
items.description:
PROFILE: Chardonnay • Full-bodied white • Rich, creamy, buttery.
PAIRS: Cream sauces, rich pasta, chicken.
Dish examples: Fettuccine Alfredo, Mezzaluna, Chicken Bryan.
WHY: Rich Chardonnay matches rich sauce — like butter meeting butter.
LINE: If they like a richer white, Rombauer is creamy and amazing with Alfredo-style dishes.

items.name: Kendall-Jackson Chardonnay
items.description:
PROFILE: Chardonnay • Medium-full body • Smooth fruit + soft oak.
PAIRS: Chicken, creamy pastas, seafood (if they want richer white).
Dish examples: Chicken Piccata, Mezzaluna, Filet & Shrimp Toscana.
WHY: Medium-bodied Chardonnay bridges seafood and creamy sauces.
LINE: This is a smooth, classic Chardonnay — great if they want a richer white without going too heavy.
